Quantum Circuit Simulator

Drag gates onto qubit lines, select a gate and add any number of controls, build arbitrary rotation gates, group subcircuits into reusable custom gates, and load standard circuits such as Grover, QFT, and a Shor period-finding circuit. Exact state-vector simulation is exponential, so large circuits are possible to draw, but simulation becomes expensive as the number of qubits grows.

Custom gates

No custom gates yet. Select a rectangle in the circuit and group it.

Standard programs

To make a controlled version of any gate, including a grouped gate: click the gate, press Add/remove controls, then click control cells in the same column. Multiple controls are supported. Grouped gates are placed as one compact block and expanded only during simulation.

Rotations accept expressions such as pi/2, -3*pi/8, or decimals. Use Import / Export to preserve the circuit together with all grouped-gate definitions.

Measurement / classical register

No measurement gates yet. Drag M onto qubit lines to perform computational-basis measurements.

Measurement-register distribution

Add measurement gates M to define a smaller classical register. Without measurement gates, the full final-state table already gives the computational-basis probabilities; press Sample shots to draw repeated basis samples.

QEC tools

Use the buttons above to decode QEC syndromes, estimate logical error rates, compare circuits, inspect stabilizers, or decompose gates.
Export circuit JSON

Grouped gate construction

This is the hidden internal circuit used during simulation. The main circuit keeps grouped gates as compact blocks. Double-click a nested grouped gate in this viewer to inspect it recursively; use Back to return. Recursive grouped-gate cycles are rejected when grouping or loading circuits.
